text: This study examined the conditions of work for the administrative judiciary in Jordan. The Jordanian litigation in administrative judiciary is currently of two levels: the Administrative Court and the Supreme Administrative Court instead of the sole Jordanian Supreme Court of Justice (previously). Therefore, litigation in the administrative judiciary came to be fair through the litigation of two judicial bodies whereby one of the conditions of a fair trial, which was addressed in the constitutions, is that a case be judged by more than one court or at more than one level. The results of this applied study, as well as the tables and illustrations that have been presented, confirmed that the number of lawsuits brought before the administrative judiciary in 2020 exceeded the number of lawsuits brought before it in 2019. Despite the fact that litigation in administrative judiciary is now of two levels, it is not sufficient to have administrative courts of the first instance all over the Kingdom, because administrative courts are currently only established in the capital. In addition to that, it is recommended that administrative courts of appeal be established in the governorates of the Kingdom. In this respect, a standard should be developed that determines the jurisdiction of administrative judicial authorities in the countries where powers are distributed over administrative disputes in more than one judicial authority, in order to know which of the normal judicial authorities in charge of some administrative disputes and in order to know the administrative courts defined by law in this regard.
explanation: The text is domain-specific and reads like a scholarly discussion, but a few sentences appear awkward and overly generalized in a way that resembles machine-generated or translated prose. This keeps AI authorship plausible but not definitive.
